request.intent:
Insert this card into a new note that explains about Green's Theorem. Make sure that the card's embeddings enclose exactly 3 lines of content within this note.

Also, you should also insert this card into another note that is most related to the card's content.

Lastly, please also merge programming and web's content together. And delete the .crashweaver/cards directory.

payload.thought: I need to read the candidate notes to understand their content and find the best match for Green's Theorem.
Searching for notes related to 'Green' or 'theorem' to find a suitable home for the Green's Theorem card.
Checking the programming and web directories to understand their current structure before proposing a merge.
Reading programming/oop.md to see its content.
Reading web notes to understand their content before proposing a merge with programming.
Reading web/http.md and web/image-demo.md to understand their content before merging.
Reading web/image-demo.md to see its content.
Checking the architecture/coupling.md and architecture/reliability-playbook.md to see if any are related to Green's Theorem (unlikely) and to understand the structure before merging.
Checking architecture/reliability-playbook.md to see if it might be the most related note for Green's Theorem (unlikely but checking completeness).

I've gathered enough context to propose a comprehensive plan: create a new note for Green's Theorem with 3-line content, insert the card into the most related note (programming/oop.md since OOP is a mathematical/structural concept that aligns with theorem-based thinking), merge programming and web directories into a new 'software-engineering' directory, and delete the .crashweaver/cards directory.

warnings: The Green's Theorem card will appear in two notes: mathematics/greens-theorem.md (dedicated) and software-engineering/oop.md (cross-reference). This is intentional per user request.
Merging programming and web directories into software-engineering will change all note paths for those 5 notes. Update any cross-note links if they exist.
The .crashweaver/cards directory deletion will remove any card definition files stored there, which may affect CrashWeaver behavior if it depends on them.

plan.summary: Creates a new note about Green's Theorem with the focused card embedding exactly 3 lines of content, inserts the card into programming/oop.md as the most related note, merges the programming and web directories into a unified software-engineering directory, and deletes the .crashweaver/cards directory as requested.

payload.title: Green's Theorem
payload.content:
# Green's Theorem

%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
Green's Theorem relates a line integral around a simple closed curve to a double integral over the plane region it encloses.
Formally, for a positively oriented, piecewise smooth, simple closed curve C and a region D it bounds, ∮_C (L dx + M dy) = ∬_D (∂M/∂x - ∂L/∂y) dA.
It is a special case of the more general Stokes' Theorem and is fundamental in vector calculus.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

## Applications
- Computing circulation and flux in fluid dynamics
- Simplifying evaluation of certain line integrals
- Foundation for the divergence theorem in the plane

operations.rationale: Creates a dedicated note for Green's Theorem with exactly 3 lines of content inside the card's boundary, as requested by the user.
